task: Build the ResearchOps research repository front page on a new branch using Nunjucks and Sass, with macros for components and design patterns. Follow-up scope added realistic D1 seed data for a populated curated repository review experience.
buildGeneratedOutputs: public/css/repository.css is generated by Sass during build and is not committed.
public/pages/repository/index.html is rendered by Nunjucks during build and is not committed.
Deeper repository pages under public/pages/repository/**/index.html are rendered by Nunjucks during build and are not committed.
Route-state coverage checks Nunjucks, Sass and renderer registration, not committed generated HTML.
decisions: Use /pages/repository/ as the canonical page route.
Add Research repository to shared service navigation.
Keep repository page content in a data module so the template remains structural.
Use page-specific Nunjucks macros for repository design patterns.
Register the Sass source in the generated CSS manifest.
Register pages/repository.njk in the GOV.UK renderer.
Remove user-facing internal rationale panels and repository status panels.
Add D1-backed repository API and migration for curated, non-PII published artefacts.
Add Airtable fallback for published repository artefacts using page-size-limited records API pagination.
Route /api/repository, /api/repository/artefacts and /api/repository/artefacts/:id through the Worker as well as the Pages function.
Add source-owned deeper repository pages for service-area, user-group, method, risk, artefact detail and curator queue destinations.
Add realistic D1 seed data: 100 published artefacts, 20 candidate records, 10 withdrawn records, facet coverage and linked recommendation tags.
Keep generated repository HTML and CSS ignored so local builds do not reintroduce committed generated outputs.
Update the Lychee workflow to build generated pages and assets before checking links, and exclude runtime repository API URLs from static link validation.
Restrict credentialed CORS on the repository Pages Function to same-origin or configured ALLOWED_ORIGINS.
Point dynamic result links at the generated generic artefact detail route and load the selected artefact by ID from the API.
Align method and maturity filters with stored D1 facet values.
Apply repository schema and seed migrations in preview deployment workflows so branch previews populate D1 instead of serving empty, on-demand-created repository tables.
Keep synthetic repository seed application scoped to preview deployment paths; production deployment is not auto-seeded with demo records.
Replace broad static browse pages with facet-driven browse interfaces for service area, user group, research method and risk/constraint.
Add a candidate artefact submission interface and API route that creates candidate records with publication gates pending, keeping them outside published repository search until review.

Hydrate candidate source project choices from /api/projects and capture the submitted evidence type so candidate records are anchored to accessible project data and not only free-text source references.
codexReviewThreadDisposition: 3369448387: legitimate P1 CORS finding on functions/api/repository/[[path]].js; fixed in 2db93d1c, replied with evidence, added a thumbs-up reaction to the original Codex comment and resolved the thread after it was outdated by the fix.

3369448388: legitimate P2 generated artefact link finding on infra/cloudflare/src/service/repository.js; fixed in 2db93d1c, replied with evidence, added a thumbs-up reaction to the original Codex comment and resolved the thread after it was outdated by the fix.

3369448391: legitimate P2 maturity filter finding on src/govuk/templates/pages/repository.njk; fixed in 2db93d1c, replied with evidence, added a thumbs-up reaction to the original Codex comment and resolved the thread after it was outdated by the fix.
Review-thread state gate checked PR #366 at head 2db93d1caa2bdba9e5307a6c221ce5d9ca63be31; all three Codex review threads were resolved and outdated after the fix.
teamConsultation: Product management confirmed the repository is a top-level authenticated evidence library, not a dashboard or raw archive.
Research Operations required publication boundaries around PII, consent scope, provenance, confidence and reuse guidance.
Content design removed internal status/rationale language from user-facing page copy and kept errors plain.
Interaction design kept stable page structure in Nunjucks, with search, filters, browse routes, published results and curator workbench as visible interface elements.
Accessibility checked GOV.UK macro use, no broken ARIA hint reference, no passive focus target and no technical error copy.
Technical architecture made D1 the primary store, Airtable the fallback source, and routed /api/repository through both Worker and Pages-function paths.
QA added route-state coverage for renderer registrations, deeper repository routes, API backing, fallback, generated-output ownership and migration ordering.

npm test -- --test-reporter=dot passed.
npm run format:check passed.
npm run generated-css:check passed.
npm run lint passed with existing warnings.
npm run validate passed.
npm run trace:coverage passed.
npm run agent:bundles:validate passed.
Follow-up focused tests after adding seed data passed.
Follow-up Lychee fix validation passed: npm run build, focused repository route-state tests and npm run format:check.
Follow-up Codex review fix validation passed: focused repository route-state tests and npm run format:check.
Follow-up operating-model correction passed: review-thread state inspection, thumbs-up reactions and thread resolution completed for all three Codex comments.
Remote PR checks passed for PR #366 after 2db93d1c, including Lychee, Node 20, Node 22, release gate, repository contract, Cloudflare Worker validation, accessibility and BDD smoke checks.
PR readiness gate checked 27 changed files; the changed-file list is plausible for repository API, D1 seed data, deeper source pages, link-check workflow, tests, docs and trace updates.

Follow-up live preview defect found: the seed migration existed in the repository, but preview deployment workflows did not apply 0014_research_repository.sql or 0015_seed_research_repository.sql; authenticated preview pages could therefore show zero repository records.
Follow-up preview seed deployment fix validation passed: focused repository route-state test and npm run validate.
Follow-up deeper page correction validation passed: focused repository route-state test, JavaScript syntax checks and npm run build.

Follow-up candidate source evidence tightening passed: focused repository route-state test, JavaScript syntax checks, npm run build, browser smoke checks against local rendered pages, npm run lint, npm run validate and npm test.
npm test -- --ci could not run because the package test script passes arguments to Node's test runner and Node rejected --ci; npm test passed instead.
package.json has no typecheck script, so no typecheck command was available to run.
residualRisks: The preview link will show seeded data after the updated preview deployment workflow finishes applying the repository migrations to the remote preview D1 database.
Production D1 still requires an explicit human-owned migration/seeding decision before synthetic data is introduced there.
Candidate creation now writes governed D1 candidate records, but full curator review actions such as approve, update and withdraw remain future workflow endpoints.
The local browser smoke-test server started on 127.0.0.1:4177; sandbox restrictions prevented terminating the process with kill after the check, so no further local server was started.
